commonIssues:
Mismatched names, addresses, or dates of birth across Aadhaar cards, PAN cards, or birth certificates often lead to application rejection.

How to fix:

  • Ensure uniform names across all IDs. Update your Aadhaar first via UIDAI if needed.

Users have reported difficulties with the website, including finding the correct sub-registrar office in their area or navigating the login/registration process.

How to fix:

  • Use the TNReginet Portal during non-peak hours and utilize their official helpdesk for persistent issues.

The requirement to bring three witnesses with proper identification, who must be physically present for the final registration, can create scheduling conflicts.

How to fix:

  • Ensure all three witnesses carry original government IDs and are present at the Sub-Registrar office.

Couples, especially those from different backgrounds or who have married without parental consent, have faced hurdles in submitting traditional evidence like wedding invitations, resulting in difficulties proving the marriage took place.

How to fix:

  • Register under the Special Marriage Act or provide an affidavit and joint photographs if traditional proofs are absent.

Sometimes, local officials or agents may wrongly insist on documents that are not required by law, such as personal IDs of parents who may not support the marriage.

How to fix:

  • Refer to the official TNReginet guidelines. Parental IDs are not legally mandatory for adults.

Under the Special Marriage Act, couples often misunderstand the 30-day waiting period requirement.

How to fix:

  • Plan for the 30-day waiting period if marrying under the Special Marriage Act. It cannot be bypassed.

Errors in the spelling of names, date of marriage, or date of birth on the final certificate are common, requiring a tedious correction process.

How to fix:

  • Apply for correction at the same Sub-Registrar office immediately with a notarized affidavit.

While it takes 7–15 working days, if there are clarifications needed regarding documents, the issuance can be delayed significantly.

How to fix:

  • Follow up physically at the office if the certificate is not generated within 15 working days.

Even if the application is filed online, both partners must attend in person with witnesses to sign the register and complete biometrics, causing issues for couples working abroad or living elsewhere.

How to fix:

  • Both partners must schedule an appointment and visit in person; remote registration is not permitted.

If not registered promptly (within 90 days), updating documents such as passports to reflect the spouse's name can become complex.

How to fix:

  • Register the marriage within 90 days. Use the certificate to apply for a Gazette name change.

If the original marriage was not legally solemnized, the registrar may refuse to register it, causing it to be deemed a "fake" marriage certificate.

How to fix:

  • Provide an affidavit sworn before a notary public detailing the marriage circumstances if it was not officially solemnized.
